title: goodenia corynocarpa
intro: Goodenia corynocarpa, also known as bush goodenia, is a shrub-like plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to make infusions and tinctures, providing health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues. Goodenia corynocarpa is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and creams, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
